steps.title: Make the exponent zero
steps.content: To show $f(1)$ directly as a coefficient, the exponential factor should become $1$ when $x=1$. That happens when the exponent is $x-1$.

steps.title: Check the matching form
steps.content:
For the form $f(x)=98(1.4)^{x-1}$, substituting $x=1$ gives
$$f(1)=98(1.4)^0=98$$
So the coefficient shown is the value of $k$. The answer is C.
